mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market and elevated recession probability, which reduces expected corporate earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decelerating inflation indicate robust consumer spending without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and inflation loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month during periods of monetary tightening demonstrates resilient supply-side activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal direction flips to negative during tightening periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue potential, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth shows no correlation with forward equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 3.5 percent year-over-year force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns dominate in more than 55 percent of instances when CPI exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts occurring alongside positive GDP growth signal synchronized demand expansion across residential construction and the broader economy, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or interaction coefficient turns negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during early-cycle expansion phases indicates improving labor utilization and accelerating earnings growth, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power or reverses direction when filtered for early-cycle expansion regimes in walk-forward validation.
